title: equisetum telmateia subsp. braunii
intro: Equisetum telmateia subsp. braunii, also known as scouring rush, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the horsetail family (Equisetaceae) and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Equisetum telmateia subsp. braunii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and has also been used in herbal remedies for its purported ability to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
